\"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n100\t        if self._zai_client is None:\n101\t            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n102\t        return self._zai_client\n103\t\n104\t    async def _get_store(self):\n105\t        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n106\t        if self.store is None:\n107\t            self.store = get_store()\n108\t        return self.store\n109\t\n110\t    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n111\t        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n112\t        if self._reload_manager is None:\n113\t            self._reload_manager = call).\"\"\"\n118\t        try:\n119\t            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n120\t        except Exception as e:\n121\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt from {self.prompt_path}: {e}\")\n122\t            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n123\t\n124\t    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n125\t        \"\"\"\n126\t        Load the urgency classification prompt from disk via the hot-reload manager.\n127\t\n128\t        prompts/urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact (registered in\n129\t        src/components/hot_reload.py). Splicing it here keeps urgency guidance\n130\t        editable independently of the segmentation prompt. Returns \"\" on failure\n131\t        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n132\t        \"\"\"\n133\t        try:\n134\t            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n135\t        except Exception as e:\n136\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n137\t            return \"\"\n138\t\n139\t    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n140\t        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n141\t        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n142\t        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n143\t        if urgency_prompt:\n144\t            system_prompt = (\n145\t                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n146\t            )\n147\t        return system_prompt\n148\t\n149\t    async def classify_utterance(\n150\t        self,\n151\t        utterance: str,\n152\t        session_id: str,\n153\t    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n154\t        \"\"\"\n155\t        Classify an utterance into intents.\n156\t\n157\t        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n158\t\n159\t        Args:\n160\t            utterance: The user utterance\n161\t            session_id: Session ID for context\n162\t\n163\t        Returns:\n164\t            List of IntentClassification objects\n165\t        \"\"\"\n166\t        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n167\t\n168\t        # Build user message with session context if available\n169\t        store = await self._get_store()\n170\t        session_context = \"\"\n171\t        try:\n172\t            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n173\t            if session:\n174\t                # Get recent intents for context\n175\t                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n176\t                if recent_intents:\n177\t                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n178\t                    for intent in recent_intents:\n179\t                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n180\t        except Exception as e:\n181\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n182\t\n183\t        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n184\t\n185\t        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n186\t\n187\t        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n188\t        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n189\t        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n190\t\n191\t        try:\n192\t            response = await client.call_simple(\n193\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n194\t                user_message=user_message,\n195\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n196\t                max_tokens=2048,\n197\t                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n198\t            )\n199\t\n200\t            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n201\t            raw = response.strip()\n202\t            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n203\t                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n204\t                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n205\t\n206\t            # Parse JSON response\n207\t            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n208\t            classifications = []\n209\t\n210\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n211\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n212\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n213\t                try:\n214\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n215\t                except ValueError:\n216\t                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n217\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n218\t\n219\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n220\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n221\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n222\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n223\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n224\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n225\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n226\t                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\"),\n227\t                )\n228\t                classifications.append(classification)\n229\t\n230\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n231\t            return classifications\n232\t\n233\t        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n234\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n235\t            # Fallback: return single status intent\n236\t            return [\n237\t                IntentClassification(\n238\t                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n239\t                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n240\t                    confidence=0.5,\n241\t                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n242\t                )\n243\t            ]\n244\t        except Exception as e:\n245\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n246\t            raise\n247\t\n248\t    async def route_utterance(\n249\t        self,\n250\t        utterance: str,\n251\t        utterance_id: str,\n252\t        session_id: str,\n253\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n254\t        \"\"\"\n255\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n256\t\n257\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n258\t\n259\t        Args:\n260\t            utterance: The user utterance\n261\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n262\t            session_id: Session ID\n263\t\n264\t        Returns:\n265\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n266\t        \"\"\"\n267\t        # Each\n307\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n308\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n309\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n310\t        # the dispatch itself.\n311\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n312\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n313\t\n314\t        try:\n315\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n316\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n317\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n318\t            else:\n319\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n320\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n321\t        except Exception:\n322\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n323\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n324\t            raise\n325\t\n326\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n327\t        return result\n328\t\n329\t    async def _persist_timings(\n330\t        self,\n331\t        intent_id: str,\n332\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n333\t    ) -> None:\n334\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. except Exception as e:\n339\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n340\t\n341\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n342\t        self,\n343\t intents → fetch + synthesize strands\n\"\"\"\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport time\nimport uuid\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n    STUCK = \"stuck\"  # Stuck intent - task blocked by circuit breaker\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass IntentClassification:\n    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n    intent_type: IntentType\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    confidence: float = 1.0\n    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n    reasoning: str = \"\"\n    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n    lookup_kind: str | None = None  # lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs'\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RoutedIntent:\n    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    classification: IntentClassification\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n    # (one LLM call per utterance) and shared across every intent thread from\n    # that utterance — see Latency Segment the utterance into \"\n    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n    \"intent objects.\"\n)\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n        self.store = store\n        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Load the router segmentation prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt from {self.prompt_path}: {e}\")\n            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n\n    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load the urgency classification prompt from disk via the hot-reload manager.\n\n        prompts/urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact (registered in\n        src/components/hot_reload.py). Splicing it here keeps urgency guidance\n        editable independently of the segmentation prompt. Returns \"\" on failure\n        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n            return \"\"\n\n    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n        if urgency_prompt:\n            system_prompt = (\n                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n            )\n        return system_prompt\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Build user message with session context if available\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        session_context = \"\"\n        try:\n            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n            if session:\n                # Get recent intents for context\n                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n                if recent_intents:\n                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n                    for intent in recent_intents:\n                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=2048,\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n            raw = response.strip()\n            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\"),\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return single status intent\n            return [\n                IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n                    confidence=0.5,\n                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n                )\n            ]\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n        \"\"\"\n        # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n        # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n        # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n        classify_start = time.monotonic()\n        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n        router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n        routed_intents = []\n        for classification in classifications:\n            # Create intent ID\n            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                intent_id=intent_id,\n                classification=classification,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                router_ms=router_ms,\n            )\n            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n        return routed_intents\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(classification.intent_type)\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            from ..registry import get_project\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n            project_cfg = (\n                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n                if classification.project_slug else None\n            )\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n\n            def _on_first_source(_source, _result) -> None:\n                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_first_source)\n            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n                timings.record(\n                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n                )\n\n            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n            required_sources_failed = [\n                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n            ]\n\n            if required_sources_failed:\n                failure_reason = f\"Required data sources failed: {', '.join(s.value for s in required_sources_failed)}\"\n                logger.error(f\"Required sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n\n                # Handle terminal failure\n                await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                    routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                    failure_reason=failure_reason,\n                    error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n                    \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n                }\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synth_start = timings.clock()\n            synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not measurable on the current\n            # call_simple path (no token stream) and is left NULL until the\n            # synthesize strand streams — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Derive result_type at write-time\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                data=synthesize_result.data,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"resolved\",\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n            # Detect terminal failure and handle appropriately\n            await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                failure_reason=str(e),\n                error_type=\"worker_crash\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Fetch/synthesize failed - terminal error\",\n            }\n\n    async def _handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        failure_reason: str,\n        error_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Handle terminal failure for a routed intent.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The intent that failed\n            failure_reason: Human-readable failure reason\n            error_type: Type of error (worker_crash, invalid_input, required_source_failure)\n            bead_ref: Associated bead reference (if applicable)\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..escalate.handler import handle_terminal_failure\n\n        # Get or create topic for failed card\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Failed: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                topic_type=\"exception\",\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to create topic for failed card: {e}\")\n            topic_id = None\n\n        # Handle terminal failure (updates intent status, stores in bead_watch, broadcasts SSE)\n        await handle_terminal_failure(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            failure_reason=failure_reason,\n            error_type=error_type,\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n        )\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n            IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n        }\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            bead_watch = await store.get_bead_watch(bead_ref)\n\n            if not bead_watch:\n                return None\n\n            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n                return {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n                }\n\n            return None\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        fence_context: dict,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a stuck card when a fenced bead is detected during intent routing.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent\n            fence_context: Fence context from _check_fence_for_bead\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with stuck card details\n        \"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime\n\n        bead_id = fence_context[\"bead_id\"]\n        refusal_reason = fence_context[\"refusal_reason\"]\n        refusal_count = fence_context[\"refusal_count\"]\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Creating stuck card for fenced bead {bead_id} \"\n            f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n\n            # Update intent type to 'stuck' and status to 'stuck'\n            await store.update_intent_type_and_status(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=\"stuck\",\n                status=\"stuck\",\n            )\n\n            # Get or create topic for this stuck card\n            topic_type = \"project\" if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else \"research\"\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Fenced: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[routed_intent.classification.project_slug] if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Link intent to topic\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Create stuck result\n            summary = f\"Task stuck — needs your input\"\n            data = {\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": f\"This task has been blocked after {refusal_count} refusals.\",\n                \"action_hint\": \"Review the bead and provide the missing information or context needed to proceed.\",\n                \"fence_detected_during\":      project_slug=routed_intent.classification.project_slug,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=summary,\n                data=data,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created stuck card {result_id} for fenced bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            # Broadcast task_stuck event via SSE\n            broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n            await broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n                    data={\n detected during intent routing\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create stuck card from fence: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": \"stuck\",\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed       \"\"\"\n        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLESS bead.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector;    # Step 1: Check for existing fenced beads in the session before escalating\n        # This detects fence events when last_refusal_reason is set on existing beads\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            fenced_beads = await store.get_fenced_beads_for_session(\n                routed_intent.session_id\n            )\n\n            # If there are fenced beads, extract fence context and create stuck card\n            if fenced_beads:\n                # Get the most recently fenced bead\n                most_recent_fenced = fenced_beads[0]\n                bead_ref = most_recent_fenced[\"bead_ref\"]\n                refusal_reason = most_recent_fenced.get(\"last_refusal_reason\", \"Fenced (no reason provided)\")\n                refusal_count = most_recent_fenced.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n                fence_context = {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": most_recent_fenced.get(\"fenced_at\"),\n                }\n\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"Detected fenced bead {bead_ref} for session {routed_intent.session_id} \"\n                    f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n                )\n\n                # Create stuck card with fence context\n                return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                    routed_intent, fence_context\n                )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking for fenced beads: {e}\")\n            # Continue to escalation if fence check fails\n\n        # Step 2: No fenced beads detected - proceed with escalation\n        # Build escalate request\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead — escalate_ms budgets formulation + validation +\n            # bf create (task-profile dispatches only; NULL for hot-path rows).\n            esc_start = timings.clock()\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n            timings.record(\"escalate_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(esc_start))\n\n            # Check if the created bead is immediately fenced (race condition:\n            # watcher may have fenced it between escalate request and now)\n            if result.bead_id:\n                fence_context = await self._check_fence_for_bead(result.bead_id)\n                if fence_context:\n                    logger.info(\n                        f\"Detected immediate fence for bead {result.bead_id} \"\n                        f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n                    )\n                    # Return stuck card instead of pending card\n                    return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                        routed_intent, fence_context\n                    )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"escalated\",\n                \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n                \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n                \"message\": f\"Escalated to bead {result.bead_id}\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalation failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to escalate to bead\",\n the caller mints in create_intent(). router_ms is shared across\n181\t-- every intent thread from the same utterance; escalate_ms is NULL for hot-path\n182\t-- dispatches; stt_ms/first_render_ms are client-reported and NULL when the\n183\t-- client never reports them. synthesize_first_token_ms is NULL until the\n184\t-- synthesize strand streams (the non-streaming call_simple path can't measure\n185\t-- first token separately) — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n186\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dispatch_timings (\n187\t    intent_id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n188\t    router_ms                 INTEGER,\n189\t    fetch_first_source_ms     INTEGER,\n190\t    fetch_total_ms            INTEGER,\n191\t    synthesize_first_token_ms INTEGER,\n192\t    synthesize_total_ms       INTEGER,\n193\t    escalate_ms               INTEGER,\n194\t    sse_emit_ms               INTEGER,\n195\t    stt_ms                    INTEGER,\n196\t    first_render_ms           INTEGER,\n197\t    created_at                INTEGER NOT NULL\n198\t);\n199\t\n200\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dispatch_timings_created ON dispatch_timings(created_at);\n201\t\n202\t-- Bead watch table: circuit breaker tracking for async beads\n203\t-- Persistence layer for the async-path circuit breaker (plan §10 The Async Path).\n204\t-- Tracks open beads watched by the BeadWatcher, recording refusal counts,\n205\t-- last refusal reasons, SLA deadlines, and fencing state. All state is persisted\n206\t-- so a watcher restart loses nothing -- breaker state lives in the database,\n207\t-- not only in memory.\n208\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bead_watch (\n209\t    bead_ref           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,  -- References bead ID (intents.bead_ref)\n210\t    refusal_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of REFUSED: comments seen\n211\t    last_refusal_reason TEXT,  -- Most recent refusal reason\n212\t    last_refusal_at    INTEGER,  -- Timestamp of most recent refusal\n213\t    comment_high_water INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT -1,  -- Latest comment index processed (-1 = none)\n214\t    sla_deadline       INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- Unix timestamp when SLA expires\n215\t    sla_flagged_at     INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when SLA was flagged (NULL if not flagged)\n216\t    fenced_at          INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when bead was fenced to status=blocked (NULL if not fenced)\n217\t    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL  -- When this watch row was created\n218\t);\n219\t\n220\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline ON bead_watch(sla_deadline);\n221\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_fenced ON bead_watch(fenced_at);\n222\t\"\"\"\n223\t\n224\t# The set of dispatch_timings timing columns record_dispatch_timings() may set.\n225\t# intent_id/created_at are handled explicitly (PK / insert-only timestamp).\n226\tDISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS = (\n227\t    \"router_ms\",\n228\t    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n229\t    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n230\t    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n231\t    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n232\t    \"escalate_ms\",\n233\t    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n234\t    \"stt_ms\",\n235\t    \"first_render_ms\",\n236\t)\n237\t\n238\t# Valid intent status values (plan §10 The Async Path: stuck/failed added for circuit breaker)\n239\tINTENT_STATUSES = ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')\n240\t\n241\t# Default SLA hours per intent type (plan §10 The Async Path: Visible Aging)\n242\t# task-profile: 6h (async bead-backed tasks)\n243\t# hot-path intents: 30s (budget is 3s, flag at 10x for aged pending)\n244\tDEFAULT_SLA_HOURS: dict[str, float] = {\n245\t    \"task-profile\": 6.0,  # 6 hours for async bead-backed tasks\n246\t    \"status\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path status intents\n247\t    \"action\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path action intents\n248\t    \"lookup\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path lookup intents\n249\t    \"brainstorm\": 0.5,   # 30 minutes for brainstorm (may need user iteration)\n250\t    \"reminder\": 24.0,    # 24 hours for reminders\n251\t}\n252\t\n253\t# Circuit breaker thresholds (plan §10 The Async Path)\n254\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_REFUSAL_THRESHOLD = 3  # Fence after N refusals\n255\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_AGE_THRESHOLD_HOURS = 24.0  # Fence after N hours without progress\n256\t\n257\t\n258\tdef derive_result_type(\n259\t    intent_type: Optional[str],\n260\t    project_slug: Optional[str],\n261\t    lookup_kind: Optional[str] = None,\n262\t) -> str:\n263\t    \"\"\"Derive the deterministic card-selector key written to ``results.result_type``.\n264\t\n265\t    - intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n266\t      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n267\t    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n268\t      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n269\t      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n270\t    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n271\t\n272\t    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n273\t    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n274\t    \"\"\"\n275\t    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n276\t    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n277\t    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n278\t        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n279\t    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n280\t        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n281\t    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n282\t\n283\t\n284\tclass SessionStore:\n285\t    \"\"\"Session store with WAL mode for concurrent access.\"\"\"\n286\t\n287\t    def __init__(self, db_path: Path):\n288\t        self.db_path = db_path\n289\t\n290\t    async def initialize(self) -> None:\n291\t        \"\"\"Initialize database with schema and WAL mode.\"\"\"\n292\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n293\t            # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n294\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n295\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n296\t\n297\t            # Create schema\n298\t            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n299\t            await db.commit()\n300\t\n301\t            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n302\t            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n303\t            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n304\t            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n305\t            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n306\t            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n307\t\n308\t    @staticmethod\n309\t    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n310\t        \"\"\"Idempotently add columns introduced after the initial schema.\"\"\"\n311\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(results)\") as cur:\n312\t            result_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n313\t        if \"result_type\" not in result_cols:\n314\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN result_type TEXT\")\n315\t        if \"card_fallback\" not in result_cols:\n316\t            await db.execute(\n317\t                \"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"\n318\t            )\n319\t\n320\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n321\t            intent_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n322\t        if \"lookup_kind\" not in intent_cols:\n323\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents ADD COLUMN lookup_kind TEXT\")\n324\t\n325\t        # Migrate intents.status CHECK constraint to include 'stuck' and 'failed'\n326\t        # SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we need to recreate the table\n327\t        # First, check if the table still has the old constraint (without stuck/failed)\n328\t        await SessionStore._migrate_intents_status_enum(db)\n329\t\n330\t        # Migrate results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\n331\t        await SessionStore._migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db)\n332\t\n333\t        # Migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\n334\t        await SessionStore._migrate_bead_watch_comment_high_water(db)\n335\t\n336\t        await db.commit()\n337\t\n338\t    @staticmethod\n339\t    async def _migrate_intents_status_enum(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n340\t        \"\"\"Migrate intents table to support 'stuck' and 'failed' status values.\n341\t\n342\t        Plan §10 The Async Path: extend intents status enum with stuck/failed.\n343\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we recreate the table.\n344\t        \"\"\"\n345\t        # Check if migration is needed by looking for 'stuck' in the schema\n346\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n347\t            cols = await cur.fetchall()\n348\t            status_col = None\n349\t            for col in cols:\n350\t                if col[1] == \"status\":\n351\t                    status_col = col\n352\t                    break\n353\t\n354\t        # If no status column or already has stuck/failed, skip migration\n355\t        if not status_col:\n356\t            return\n357\t\n358\t        # Check if the CHECK constraint already includes stuck/failed\n359\t        # by inspecting the SQL for the intents table\n360\t        async with db.execute(\n361\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='intents'\"\n362\t        ) as cur:\n363\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n364\t            if table_sql and (\"'stuck'\" in table_sql[0] or \"'failed'\" in table_sql[0]):\n365\t                # Already migrated\n366\t                return\n367\t\n368\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new constraint\n369\t        logger.info(\"Migrating intents.status to include 'stuck' and 'failed' values\")\n370\t\n371\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n372\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n373\t\n374\t        try:\n375\t            # Create new table with updated constraint\n376\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n377\t                CREATE TABLE intents_new (\n378\t                    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n379\t                    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n380\t                    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n381\t                    topic_id     TEXT,\n382\t                    project_slug TEXT,\n383\t                    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n384\t                    lookup_kind  TEXT,\n385\t                    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n386\t                    bead_ref     TEXT,\n387\t                    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n388\t                    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n389\t                    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n390\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n391\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n392\t                )\n393\t            \"\"\")\n394\t\n395\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n396\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n397\t                INSERT INTO intents_new\n398\t                SELECT id, utterance_id, session_id, topic_id, project_slug,\n399\t                       intent_type, lookup_kind, status, bead_ref, created_at, resolved_at\n400\t                FROM intents\n401\t            \"\"\")\n402\t\n403\t            # Recreate indexes\n404\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_session\")\n405\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_topic\")\n406\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_status\")\n407\t\n408\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_session ON intents_new(session_id)\")\n409\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_topic ON intents_new(topic_id)\")\n410\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_status ON intents_new(status)\")\n411\t\n412\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n413\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE intents\")\n414\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents_new RENAME TO intents\")\n415\t\n416\t            await db.commit()\n417\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: intents.status now supports 'stuck' and 'failed'\")\n418\t\n419\t        except Exception as e:\n420\t            await db.rollback()\n421\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate intents.status: {e}\")\n422\t            raise\n423\t\n424\t    @staticmethod\n425\t    async def _migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n426\t        \"\"\"Migrate results table to allow NULL intent_id for monitoring-originated results.\n427\t\n428\t        Plan §10 Bead Watcher: monitoring-originated results have intent_id=NULL.\n429\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN to drop NOT NULL, so we recreate the table.\n430\t        \"\"\"\n431\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the results table schema\n432\t        async with db.execute(\n433\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='results'\"\n434\t        ) as cur:\n435\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n436\t            if not table_sql:\n437\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n438\t\n439\t            # If intent_id is already nullable (NOT NULL not present), skip migration\n440\t            if \"intent_id   TEXT,\" in table_sql[0] or \"intent_id TEXT,\" in table_sql[0]:\n441\t                if \"NOT NULL\" not in table_sql[0].split(\"intent_id\")[1].split(\",\")[0]:\n442\t                    # Already migrated\n443\t                    return\n444\t\n445\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with nullable intent_id\n446\t        logger.info(\"Migrating results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\")\n447\t\n448\t        # Copy data from old table to new table - explicit column mapping\n478\t            # to handle different column orders between old and new schemas\n479\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n480\t                INSERT INTO results_new (id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, data, urgency, result_type, card_fallback, created_at, surfaced_at, acked_at, previous_result_id, diff_summary, diff_data)\n481\t                SELECT id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, data, urgency,\n482\t                       COALESCE(result_type, 'default') AS result_type,\n483\t                       COALESCE(card_fallback, 0) AS card_fallback,\n484\t                       created_at, surfaced_at, acked_at, previous_result_id, diff_summary, diff_data\n485\t                FROM results\n486\t            \"\"\")\n487\t\n488\t            # With DEFAULT -1, no comments are skipped\n516\t        initially, and only comments with index > comment_high_water are processed.\n517\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN DEFAULT, so we recreate the table.\n518\t        \"\"\"\n519\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the bead_watch table schema\n520\t        async with db.execute(\n521\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='bead_watch'\"\n522\t        ) as cur:\n523\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n524\t            if not table_sql:\n525\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n526\t\n527\t            # If comment_high_water already has DEFAULT -1, skip migration\n528\t            if \"DEFAULT -1\" in table_sql[0]:\n529\t                # Already migrated\n530\t                return\n531\t\n532\t        # Copy data from old table to new table\n555\t            # Existing rows with comment_high_water=0 are migrated as-is\n556\t            # (they represent \"comment 0 has been processed\", which is valid)\n557\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n558\t                INSERT INTO bead_watch_new\n559\t                SELECT * FROM bead_watch\n560\t            \"\"\")\n561\t\n562\t            # aiosqlite uses connection-per-context, so just ensure checkpoint\n583\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n584\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)\")\n585\t\n586\t    # Session operations\n587\t    async def create_session(self, session_id: str | None = None) -> str:\n588\t        \"\"\"Create a new session and return its ID.\"\"\"\n589\t        if not session_id:\n590\t            session_id = str(uuid4())\n591\t        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n592\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n593\t            await db.execute(\n594\t                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO sessions (id, created_at, last_active) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\",\n595\t                (session_id, now, now)\n596\t            )\n597\t            await db.commit()\n598\t        return session_id\n599\t\n600\t    async def get_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n601\t        \"\"\"Get session by ID.\"\"\"\n602\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n603\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n604\t            async with db.execute(\n605\t                \"SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n606\t            ) as cursor:\n607\t                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n608\t                return dict(row) if row else None\n609\t\n610\t    async def update_session_activity(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n611\t        \"\"\"Update session last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n612\t        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n613\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n614\t            await db.execute(\n615\t                \"UPDATE sessions SET last_active = ? Returns a removal summary.\n631\t\n632\t        Used by test teardown (DELETE /api/v1/sessions/{id}) to guarantee a\n633\t        session and all its topics/results/intents/utterances/surfaces/signal\n634\t        rows are gone. Note: SQLite FK ``ON DELETE CASCADE`` is *not* enforced\n635\t        here (``PRAGMA foreign_keys`` is never enabled), so child rows are\n636\t        deleted explicitly, in dependency order, within one transaction.\n637\t\n638\t        The topics table references sessions with ``ON DELETE SET NULL``, so\n639\t        topics are *not* cascade-removed even if FKs were on — deleting them\n640\t        explicitly is required for a true clean teardown.\n641\t\n642\t        Returns ``{\"session_removed\": 0|1, \"topics_removed\": <int>}``.\n643\t        \"\"\"\n644\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n645\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n646\t\n647\t            # Count what we're about to remove (before deleting).\n648\t            async with db.execute(\n649\t                \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM topics WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n650\t            ) as cur:\n651\t                topics_removed = (await cur.fetchone())[0]\n652\t            async with db.execute(\n653\t                \"SELECT 1 FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n654\t            ) as cur:\n655\t                session_removed = 1 if await cur.fetchone() else 0\n656\t\n657\t            # Children first, parents last (matches FK dependency order).\n658\t            await db.execute(\n659\t                \"DELETE FROM feedback_signals WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n660\t            )\n661\t            await db.execute(\n662\t                \"DELETE FROM results WHERE session_id = ?\", Ensure the row exists (created_at set once, on first write).\n1335\t            await db.execute(\n1336\t                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dispatch_timings (intent_id, created_at) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n1337\t                (intent_id, now),\n1338\t            )\n1339\t            if cols:\n1340\t                set_clause = \", \".join(f\"{c} = ?\" for c in cols)\n1341\t                params = [*cols.values(), intent_id]\n1342\t                await db.execute(\n1343\t                    f\"UPDATE dispatch_timings SET {set_clause} WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n1344\t                    params,\n1345\t                )\n1346\t            await db.commit()\n1347\t\n1348\t    async def get_dispatch_timings(self, intent_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n1349\t        \"\"\"Get the dispatch_timings row for one intent thread, or None.\"\"\"\n1350\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1351\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n1352\t            async with db.execute(\n1353\t                \"SELECT * FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n1354\t                (intent_id,),\n1355\t            ) as cursor:\n1356\t                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n1357\t                return dict(row) if row else None\n1358\t\n1359\t    async def get_latency_percentiles(self, since: int | None = None) -> dict:\n1360\t        \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per stage across dispatch_timings rows.\n1361\t\n1362\t        Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n1363\t        that has at least one non-NULL value. NULLs are skipped per stage (so a\n1364\t        stage not yet captured for any dispatch simply doesn't appear). Pass\n1365\t        ``since`` (a unix timestamp) to window to recent dispatches only — the\n1366\t        latency-baseline bead consumes the un-windowed call to fill the plan's\n1367\t        global Measured p50/p95 columns.\n1368\t\n1369\t        Percentiles are nearest-rank (ceil(q/100 * n)), computed in Python\n1370\t        because SQLite has no built-in percentile.\n1371\t        \"\"\"\n1372\t        from ..instrument.timings import percentiles\n1373\t\n1374\t        result: dict[str, dict] = {}\n1375\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1376\t            for stage in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS:\n1377\t                if since is not None:\n1378\t                    query = (\n1379\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1380\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= ? \"\n1381\t                        f\"ORDER BY {stage}\"\n1382\t                    )\n1383\t                    params: tuple = (since,)\n1384\t                else:\n1385\t                    query = (\n1386\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1387\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL ORDER BY {stage}\"\n1388\t                    )\n1389\t                    params = ()\n1390\t                async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n1391\t                    values = [row[0] for row in await cursor.fetchall()]\n1392\t                if not values:\n1393\t                    continue\n1394\t                pct = percentiles(values, qs=(50, 95))\n1395\t                result[stage] = {\n1396\t                    \"p50\": pct[50],\n1397\t                    \"p95\": pct[95],\n1398\t                    \"count\": len(values),\n1399\t                }\n1400\t        return result\n1401\t\n1402\t    async def get_session_feedback_summary(\n1403\t        self,\n1404\t        session_id: str,\n1405\t        since: int | None = None,\n1406\t    ) -> dict:\n1407\t        \"\"\"Get a summary of feedback signals for a session.\"\"\"\n1408\t        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n1409\t\n1410\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1411\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n1412\t\n1413\t            # Get signal counts by type\n1414\t            since_clause = since or one_hour_ago\n1415\t            async with db.execute(\n1416\t                \"\"\"SELECT signal_type, COUNT(*) as count\n1417\t                   FROM feedback_signals\n1418\t                   WHERE session_id = ? generic fallback card). 0 when a real component rendered it (card_cache holds the rendered HTML).\n    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n    acked_at    INTEGER,\n    previous_result_id TEXT,  -- Link to previous result for diff\n    diff_summary TEXT,  -- Human-readable diff summary\n    diff_data    TEXT,  -- JSON: detailed field diffs\n    FOREIGN KEY ON results(topic_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_created ON results(created_at);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_previous ON results(previous_result_id);\n\n-- Topics: persistent concerns organizing intents and results\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS topics (\n    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    label        TEXT NOT NULL,\n    type         TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(type IN ('project', 'research', 'personal', 'exception', 'compound')) DEFAULT 'adhoc',\n    project_slugs TEXT,  -- JSON array\n    scope        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(scope IN ('session', 'cross-session', feedback_signals(processed);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_result ON feedback_signals(result_id);\n\n-- Per-stage latency capture for every dispatch (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n-- Keyed by the intent *thread* id (routed_intent.intent_id — the same id the\n-- fetch/synthesize/escalate strands and the results row use), NOT the\n-- intents.id the caller mints in create_intent(). router_ms is shared across\n-- every intent thread from the same utterance; escalate_ms is NULL for hot-path\n-- dispatches; stt_ms/first_render_ms are client-reported and NULL when the\n-- client never reports them. synthesize_first_token_ms is NULL until the\n-- synthesize strand streams (the non-streaming call_simple path can't measure\n-- first token separately) — see src/instrument/timings.py.\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dispatch_timings (\n    intent_id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    router_ms                 INTEGER,\n    fetch_first_source_ms
